question_text:
Fruit Into Baskets

You are visiting a farm that has a single row of fruit trees arranged from left to right. The trees are represented by an integer array fruits where fruits[i] is the type of fruit the ith tree produces.

You want to collect as much fruit as possible. However, the owner has some strict rules that you must follow:

  • You only have two baskets, and each basket can only hold a single type of fruit. There is no limit on the amount of fruit each basket can hold.
  • Starting from any tree of your choice, you must pick exactly one fruit from every tree (including the start tree) while moving to the right. The picked fruits must fit in one of your baskets.
  • Once you reach a tree with fruit that cannot fit in your baskets, you must stop.

Given the integer array fruits, return the maximum number of fruits you can pick.
input_format: A single line containing space-separated integers representing the array `fruits`.
output_format: A single integer denoting the maximum number of fruits you can pick.
explanation:
This problem requires us to find the length of the longest contiguous subarray that contains at most 2 distinct integer values. A sliding window technique is optimal here. We use two pointers, `left` and `right`, to denote the current subarray bounds. As we expand the window by moving `right`, we utilize a Hash Map (dictionary) to store the occurrences (counts) of each fruit type currently in the window. 

If the size of the Hash Map exceeds 2 (meaning we have gathered 3 distinct kinds of fruits), our current subarray is invalid. We must then shrink the window from the left. We decrement the count of the fruit at the `left` pointer and advance the `left` pointer. Crucially, if the count of a fruit type drops precisely to zero, we completely delete that fruit from the Hash Map. We continue shrinking the window until the Hash Map size is exactly 2 again. At each step where the window is valid, we record the max sequence length seen (`right - left + 1`), providing an overall O(N) optimized solution.
